name: Alexandria City Public Schools
districtFact: Alexandria City Public Schools serves families in Alexandria, Virginia, immediately across the Potomac from Washington, DC and near Reagan National Airport. For calendar planning, ACPS is notable for districtwide no-school days, teacher work days, professional learning days, conference days, preschool start dates, and two-hour early-dismissal windows that matter for Northern Virginia commuter families.

about.title: About Alexandria City Public Schools
about.content: Alexandria City Public Schools serves students in the independent City of Alexandria, Virginia. The district publishes a traditional academic calendar for families with first and last day dates, preschool start dates, no-school holidays, parent-teacher conference dates, teacher work days, professional learning days, cultural observance guidance, and high school final exam early-dismissal windows. For many ACPS households, the practical planning issue is not only major breaks; it is also single-day closures and two-hour dismissals that affect pickup, after-school care, federal-office commute timing, and sibling schedules.

about.title: How to Use This ACPS Calendar
about.content: This page summarizes districtwide traditional-calendar dates from the official ACPS family calendar. Families should still confirm school-specific events, program calendars, athletics, and same-day weather or emergency changes through ACPS and their assigned school.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Alexandria Families
content: Track both full no-school days and two-hour early-dismissal windows because ACPS uses early release for conferences, high school final exams, and afternoon teacher work time.
Preschool at the ACPS Early Childhood Center and VPI classes starts after the K-12 first day, so preschool families should use the separate start date listed in the official calendar.
ACPS lists cultural observance days separately from no-school days. Treat those as regular school days unless the key dates section marks them as no school.
Check the official ACPS calendar page before finalizing travel or childcare plans because calendars can be amended after approval.

compareIntro: Compare the ACPS traditional calendar with nearby Northern Virginia district calendars currently available on MySchoolDates. Dates shown include the first day, last day, and spring break.

customSections.label: No School Days and Student Closures
customSections.content: ACPS families should track more than the three major break windows. The 2026-2027 traditional calendar includes standalone no-school dates for conferences, teacher work days, professional learning days, and district holidays, plus several two-hour early-dismissal dates that still require afternoon planning.

groups.label: Dates to review beyond major breaks
items: Conference days: ACPS lists conference-related no-school dates and two-hour early dismissals separately, so families should not rely only on holiday and break ranges.
Teacher work days and professional learning days: students do not attend on these dates, but they may not appear as holidays in generic calendar apps.
Cultural and religious observances: ACPS identifies observances separately; a weekday observance is a regular school day unless the key date is also marked as no school.
High school final exam early dismissals: June early-dismissal windows can affect transportation, work schedules, and after-school supervision even though school is still in session.

customSections.label: Teacher Work Days and Professional Learning Days
customSections.content: Teacher work days and professional learning days are among the easiest ACPS dates to miss because they are not long breaks and may sit next to regular school weeks. Add them to a personal calendar separately from Thanksgiving, Winter Break, and Spring Break.

groups.label: Why these dates matter
items: They are no-school days for students even though staff may report for planning, grade preparation, conferences, or professional learning.
They can create single-day childcare needs in October, November, January, and April depending on the school year.
They are different from two-hour early-dismissal dates, when students attend part of the day.
School-level announcements remain the final source for program-specific schedules, preschool details, and weather-related changes.

customSections.label: How We Reviewed This ACPS Calendar
customSections.content: The MySchoolDates Research Team reviewed the ACPS 2026-2027 traditional calendar against the official district family calendar and PDF before publishing this page.

groups.label: Review method
items: Checked the official ACPS calendar page and the published 2026-2027 Traditional Calendar PDF.
Cross-checked first day, last day, major breaks, no-school days, teacher work days, professional learning days, conference dates, and early dismissals.
Kept cultural observance references separate from no-school dates unless ACPS marks the date as a closure.
Linked the official source and correction process so families can verify revisions before making critical plans.

districtFaqs.q: Which ACPS calendar does this page summarize?
districtFaqs.a: This page summarizes the Alexandria City Public Schools Traditional Academic Calendar for Families, including first day, last day, no-school days, major breaks, preschool start dates, and early-dismissal windows.

districtFaqs.q: Do ACPS preschool students start on the same day as K-12 students?
districtFaqs.a: No. ACPS lists a separate first day for Preschool at the ACPS Early Childhood Center and Virginia Preschool Initiative classes. Check the year page for the exact preschool start date.

districtFaqs.q: Are ACPS cultural observance days always no-school days?
districtFaqs.a: No. ACPS identifies cultural observance days separately and states that weekday observances are regular school days unless also listed under key dates as no school.

districtFaqs.q: Where should families verify last-minute ACPS changes?
districtFaqs.a: Use the official Alexandria City Public Schools calendar page and school-level announcements for final confirmation. MySchoolDates is an independent reference and is not affiliated with ACPS.

districtFaqs.q: Does ACPS have teacher work days when students do not attend?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. ACPS lists teacher work days and professional learning days on the traditional calendar. Students do not attend on those dates, so families should add them separately from holidays and major breaks.

districtFaqs.q: What are ACPS no-school days in 2026-2027?
districtFaqs.a: ACPS no-school days include district holidays, conference days, teacher work days, professional learning days, and major recess periods such as Thanksgiving/Fall Break, Winter Break, and Spring Break. This page separates those dates from early dismissals and cultural observances.

districtFaqs.q: Does ACPS have school on Election Day 2026?
districtFaqs.a: No. The ACPS 2026-2027 traditional calendar lists Tuesday, November 3, 2026 as Election Day / Professional Learning Day, which is a no-school day for students.

districtFaqs.q: How many days are in the ACPS 2026-2027 school year?
districtFaqs.a: The ACPS 2026-2027 traditional calendar lists 180 instructional days between Monday, August 24, 2026 and Thursday, June 17, 2027.

districtFaqs.q: When does ACPS Winter Break end in 2027?
districtFaqs.a: ACPS Winter Break ends Friday, January 1, 2027. Students return after Winter Break on Monday, January 4, 2027.
